summary: P1/P2 Features Testing Complete: User Reporting System APIs fully functional, Email confirmation implemented (logs error in dev mode - expected Resend limitation), Frontend Report Modal exists but auth state issue prevents display on pro profile page

ui_bugs.issue: Report button (flag icon) not visible on professional profile page even when logged in. The auth state (isAuthenticated) is not persisting correctly when navigating to /pro/[id] page via direct URL. Token IS stored in localStorage but React component state doesn't recognize logged-in user.
ui_bugs.root_cause: Navigation to pro profile page loses Zustand auth state. The checkAuth() function in authStore.ts may not be called or completing before the component renders.

action_items: Fix auth state persistence on pro profile page - ensure isAuthenticated is true when user has valid token in localStorage/AsyncStorage
Consider calling checkAuth() in _layout.tsx or using a higher-order component to ensure auth state is loaded before rendering protected components
critical_code_review_comments: Backend APIs for user reporting are fully functional (GET /api/reports/reasons, POST /api/reports, GET /api/admin/reports, PUT /api/admin/reports/{id}/review)
Email confirmation for pro applications is implemented correctly with proper error handling - Resend limitation in dev mode is expected
ReportModal.tsx component has proper data-testid attributes and reason fetching logic
The condition in pro/[id].tsx line 196: isAuthenticated && user?.id !== id - the isAuthenticated is false on page load despite token being present

success_rate.frontend: PARTIAL - Report Modal code exists but button not visible due to auth state issue

backend_reports_api.GET_reports_reasons: PASSED - Returns 6 reasons with French labels
backend_reports_api.GET_admin_reports: PASSED - Returns reports list with stats

email_confirmation.pro_application_email: IMPLEMENTED - Logs error in dev mode (expected Resend limitation: can only send to verified domain)
email_confirmation.email_template: Beautiful HTML template with application recap

frontend_report_modal.button_visible: NO - Auth state issue prevents button from rendering

stripe_commission.25_percent_configured: YES - Commission is configured via emergentintegrations Stripe checkout

seed_data_creation: Created test user 'reporter_test@test.com' and several test reports during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend reporting APIs work correctly. The frontend Report button on pro profile page is not showing because the auth state (isAuthenticated from useAuthStore) is not being properly restored when navigating to /pro/[id] pages. The token IS in localStorage but the Zustand store's isAuthenticated remains false. Main agent needs to ensure checkAuth() is called and completes before rendering the pro/[id].tsx component.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Report button not visible on pro profile page
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: When navigating to /pro/[user_id] URL, the React component renders before the async checkAuth() function in authStore.ts completes. This leaves isAuthenticated as false even when the user has a valid token.
reproduction_steps: 3. Observe that report button (flag icon) is not visible in header
5. The issue is that useAuthStore().isAuthenticated returns false
rca_of_the_issue.suggested_fix: Ensure checkAuth() is called and awaited in the app's root layout or use a loading state while auth is being checked. The pro/[id].tsx component should show a loading indicator until isLoading is false in the auth store.
